title: Stephanotis isaura
taxonomy: Stephanotis isaura is classified within the family Asclepiadaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or milkweed family. However, Asclepiadaceae is now often considered to be a subfamily within the Apocynaceae family. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes over 5,000 species. Stephanotis isaura is a species of flowering plant native to tropical Africa. It is often grown as an ornamental for its fragrant, star-shaped flowers.
uses: Stephanotis isaura is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It has been employed to treat fever, rheumatism, and other health conditions. The plant contains compounds that have been reported to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. These properties may contribute to its potential therapeutic applications. However, further research is necessary to fully understand and confirm the medicinal uses of Stephanotis isaura.

side_effects: Stephanotis isaura, also known as the madagascar jasmine, contains a toxic compound called stephanostine, which can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea if ingested in large amounts.
